title: NIS2 One-Month Final Incident Report Tracking

compliance.assurance_rationale: NIS2 Art.23(4) — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIS2 Art.23(4) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.2.8: NIS2 One-Month Final Incident Report Tracking.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.2.8' running on notable macro, index=itsm sourcetype="snow:incident" (PIR/RCA records), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928)
spl:
`notable` urgency IN ("high","critical") status="Closed" earliest=-60d
| eval days_since_close=round((now()-_time)/86400, 1)
| eval final_report_due=if(days_since_close>=30, 1, 0)
| eval final_report_approaching=if(days_since_close>=21 AND days_since_close<30, 1, 0)
| lookup pir_completion_lookup notable_id AS event_id OUTPUT pir_status, pir_date, root_cause_documented
| fillnull value="NOT_SUBMITTED" pir_status
| where (final_report_due=1 AND pir_status!="Complete") OR final_report_approaching=1
| table _time, rule_name, urgency, owner, days_since_close, pir_status, root_cause_documented, final_report_due
| sort - final_report_due, - days_since_close
description: Article 23(4) requires a comprehensive final report within one month of the incident notification, containing root cause analysis, detailed description, mitigation measures applied, and cross-border impact assessment. This use case tracks whether closed significant incidents have completed post-incident reviews within the mandated timeframe.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIS2 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes itsm and sourcetypes snow:incident. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Create `pir_completion_lookup` linking notable IDs to post-incident review (PIR) records from ServiceNow or Confluence; (2) alert at 21 days for incidents without started PIRs; (3) escalate at 30 days for overdue final reports; (4) require root cause analysis, timeline, mitigation actions, and cross-border assessment fields before marking PIR as complete. Indexes required: `index=itsm`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=snow:incident`.
visualization: Table (overdue final reports), Single value (PIRs due this week), Bar chart (PIR status distribution), Timeline (incident-to-PIR lifecycle). Key fields: `days_since_close`, `final_report_approaching`, `final_report_due`, `owner`, `pir_status`, `root_cause_documented`.
knownFalsePositives: Reports may queue during major incidents while details are confirmed, or during initial 24h triage.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `` `notable` `` macro, `index=itsm` `sourcetype="snow:incident"` (PIR/RCA records).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Create `pir_completion_lookup` linking notable IDs to post-incident review (PIR) records from ServiceNow or Confluence; (2) alert at 21 days for incidents without started PIRs; (3) escalate at 30 days for overdue final reports; (4) require root cause analysis, timeline, mitigation actions, and cross-border assessment fields before marking PIR as complete.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
`notable` urgency IN ("high","critical") status="Closed" earliest=-60d
| eval days_since_close=round((now()-_time)/86400, 1)
| eval final_report_due=if(days_since_close>=30, 1, 0)
| eval final_report_approaching=if(days_since_close>=21 AND days_since_close<30, 1, 0)
| lookup pir_completion_lookup notable_id AS event_id OUTPUT pir_status, pir_date, root_cause_documented
| fillnull value="NOT_SUBMITTED" pir_status
| where (final_report_due=1 AND pir_status!="Complete") OR final_report_approaching=1
| table _time, rule_name, urgency, owner, days_since_close, pir_status, root_cause_documented, final_report_due
| sort - final_report_due, - days_since_close
```

Understanding this SPL

**NIS2 One-Month Final Incident Report Tracking (Art. 23(4))** — Article 23(4) requires a comprehensive final report within one month of the incident notification, containing root cause analysis, detailed description, mitigation measures applied, and cross-border impact assessment. This use case tracks whether closed significant incidents have completed post-incident reviews within the mandated timeframe.

Documented **Data sources**: `` `notable` `` macro, `index=itsm` `sourcetype="snow:incident"` (PIR/RCA records). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Invokes macro `notable` — in Search, use the UI or expand to inspect the underlying SPL.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **days_since_close** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **final_report_due** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **final_report_approaching** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Fills null values with `fillnull`.
• Filters the current rows with `where (final_report_due=1 AND pir_status!="Complete") OR final_report_approaching=1` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Output fields: _time, days_since_close, final_report_approaching, final_report_due, owner, pir_status, root_cause_documented, rule_name.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare sample results with the ES Incident Review queue and analyst outcomes so counts, severities, and fields match what your security operations team triages in production.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (overdue final reports), Single value (PIRs due this week), Bar chart (PIR status distribution), Timeline (incident-to-PIR lifecycle).
grandmaExplanation: We stay on top of one-month final incident report tracking — so we can show we run security operations and reporting the way the cyber rules that apply to us require.
